figures.caption: Fig. 1: ETac: a lightweight and efficient tactile simulation framework. (a) ETac estimates elastomer surface deformations with fidelity comparable to that of FEM. (b) It enables large-area tactile sensing for dexterous manipulators while supporting large-scale RL training.
Fig. 6: Demonstrations of Blind Grasping Skill. The process of successfully grasping 4 different objects over time.
Fig. 7: Comparison of RL training performance.

Fig. 2: Overview of the ETac pipeline. The manipulator’s surface mesh is discretized into nodes, whose contact dynamics are learned by a hybrid deformation propagation model. We calibrate the propagation model using data from FEM simulation to ensure fidelity. The computed displacement field of the nodes serves as the tactile input to the RL policy during skill learning.

Fig. 3: Computational process of the propagation model. Displacements of passive nodes (nodes not in direct contact) are estimated using a decay-based model combined with a residual correction network.

Fig. 4: Comparison of elastomer surface displacement fields estimated by different methods. Arrows show node-wise displacement direction and magnitude, with color shifting to red as z-axis displacement increases.

Fig. 5: Predicting signals of real sensors. (a) Paired data collection in real and simulated settings. (b) Comparison of sensor output predictions using ETac and FEM data. “Unseen Trajectory” denotes novel loading patterns with seen indenters.
